flying: Can only be blocked by creatures with flying or reach.
first strike: Deals combat damage before creatures without first strike.
double strike: Deals both first-strike and regular combat damage.
deathtouch: Any amount of damage it deals to a creature is enough to destroy it.
haste: Can attack and use tap abilities the turn it comes under your control.
hexproof: Can't be the target of spells or abilities your opponents control.
shroud: Can't be the target of any spells or abilities.
indestructible: Can't be destroyed by damage or effects that say 'destroy'.
lifelink: Damage it deals also causes you to gain that much life.
menace: Can't be blocked except by two or more creatures.
trample: Excess combat damage beyond a blocker's toughness is dealt to the player or planeswalker.
vigilance: Attacking doesn't cause it to tap.
flash: You may cast it any time you could cast an instant.
prowess: Whenever you cast a noncreature spell, this gets +1/+1 until end of turn.
ward: Spells/abilities opponents target it with cost an additional amount (mana, life, etc.) or are countered.
protection: Can't be damaged, enchanted, equipped, blocked, or targeted by anything with the named quality.
intimidate: Can't be blocked except by artifact creatures and creatures that share a color with it.
fear: Can't be blocked except by artifact and/or black creatures.
banding: Aids blocking; you choose how damage is assigned when it bands with attackers/blockers.
cascade: When you cast it, exile cards from your library until you hit a cheaper nonland card; you may cast it free.
convoke: You may tap creatures to help pay its cost (each pays one generic or one of its colors).
cycling: Pay the cycling cost and discard it to draw a card.
dredge: Instead of drawing, you may mill that many cards and return this from your graveyard to hand.
echo: Pay its echo cost at your next upkeep or sacrifice it.
equip: Pay the equip cost at sorcery speed to attach this Equipment to a creature you control.
exalted: Whenever a creature attacks alone, it gets +1/+1 until end of turn for each exalted.
fabricate: When it enters, put that many +1/+1 counters on it or create that many 1/1 Servo tokens.
flashback: You may cast it from your graveyard for its flashback cost, then exile it.
kicker: You may pay an optional additional cost as you cast it for a bonus effect.
landfall: Triggers an effect whenever a land enters the battlefield under your control.
morph: You may cast it face down as a 2/2 for {3}, then turn it face up for its morph cost.
myriad: When it attacks, create a tapped, attacking token copy of it for each opponent other than the defending player; exile them end of combat.
populate: Create a token copy of a creature token you control.
proliferate: For each permanent/player with a counter, you may add one more of a kind already there.
scry: Look at the top N cards of your library; put any on the bottom and the rest back on top in any order.
surveil: Look at the top N cards; put any into your graveyard and the rest back on top in any order.
storm: When you cast it, copy it for each spell cast before it this turn.
suspend: Exile it with time counters; remove one each upkeep, then cast it free when the last is removed.
threshold: Gains a bonus as long as seven or more cards are in your graveyard.
affinity: Costs {1} less to cast for each artifact you control (or other named thing).
annihilator: When it attacks, the defending player sacrifices that many permanents.
infect: Deals damage to creatures as -1/-1 counters and to players as poison counters.
miracle: If it's the first card you drew this turn, you may cast it for its cheaper miracle cost.
modular: Enters with that many +1/+1 counters; when it dies, move them to a target artifact creature.
monstrosity: Pay a cost to put +1/+1 counters on it and make it monstrous (a one-time switch).
persist: When it dies, if it had no -1/-1 counter, return it with a -1/-1 counter.
undying: When it dies, if it had no +1/+1 counter, return it with a +1/+1 counter.
phasing: It phases out (treated as not existing) and back in; phased-out permanents don't untap normally.
rebound: If cast from hand, exile it instead of graveyard; you may cast it free next upkeep.
riot: Enters with your choice of a +1/+1 counter or haste.
soulbond: You may pair it with another unpaired creature when either enters; paired creatures share a bonus.
undergrowth: Effects that scale with the number of creature cards in your graveyard.
wither: Deals damage to creatures in the form of -1/-1 counters.
amass: Put +1/+1 counters on an Army you control (creating a 0/0 Army first if needed).
adapt: If it has no +1/+1 counters, pay a cost to put N +1/+1 counters on it.
afterlife: When it dies, create that many 1/1 black-and-white Spirit tokens with flying.
ascend: If you control ten or more permanents, you get the city's blessing for the rest of the game.
mentor: When it attacks, put a +1/+1 counter on a target attacking creature with lesser power.
escape: You may cast it from your graveyard by paying its escape cost and exiling cards from your graveyard.
jump-start: You may cast it from your graveyard by also discarding a card, then exile it.
spectacle: You may cast it for its cheaper spectacle cost if an opponent lost life this turn.
mutate: Cast it onto a non-Human creature you own to merge them into one mutated creature.
companion: If your starting deck meets its condition, you may put it from outside the game into your hand once.
blitz: Cast it cheaper with haste; it's sacrificed at end of turn, but you draw a card when it dies.
casualty: As you cast it, you may sacrifice a creature of the listed power to copy the spell.
connive: Draw a card, then discard a card; if you discarded a nonland, put a +1/+1 counter on this.
disturb: You may cast it from your graveyard transformed for its disturb cost.
training: When it attacks with a creature of greater power, put a +1/+1 counter on it.
backup: When it enters, put N +1/+1 counters on a creature; if another, it also gains this card's abilities.
bargain: You may sacrifice an artifact, enchantment, or token as you cast it for a bonus.
disguise: Cast it face down as a 2/2 with ward {2}; turn it face up for its disguise cost.
plot: Pay to exile it from hand; cast it for free on a later turn (sorcery speed).
saddle: Tap other creatures with total power N to saddle this Mount, enabling its saddled abilities.
offspring: Pay an extra cost as you cast it to also create a 1/1 token copy.
extort: Whenever you cast a spell, you may pay {W/B} to drain each opponent for 1 life.
devotion: Counts colored mana symbols among the mana costs of permanents you control.
changeling: Has every creature type at all times.
investigate: Create a colorless Clue artifact token with '{2}, Sacrifice this artifact: Draw a card.'
explore: Reveal the top card; if it's a land put it into your hand, otherwise put a +1/+1 counter on this and put the card back or into your graveyard.
bolster: Put N +1/+1 counters on the creature you control with the least toughness.
support: Put a +1/+1 counter on each of up to N target creatures.
goad: Forces the creature to attack each combat if able, and a player other than you if able.
manifest: Put the top card of your library onto the battlefield face down as a 2/2; if it's a creature you may turn it face up for its mana cost.
foretell: Pay {2} to exile this from hand face down; cast it later for its foretell cost.
boast: Activate its boast ability only if this creature attacked this turn (once per turn).
cleave: You may pay the cleave cost to cast it without the bracketed text.
demonstrate: When you cast it, you may copy it, and if you do an opponent also copies it.
toxic: Combat damage to a player also gives them that many poison counters (10 poison loses the game).
incubate: Create an Incubator token with N +1/+1 counters; later pay {2} to transform it into a 0/0 Phyrexian artifact creature with the counters.
convert / transform: Turn the permanent to its other face.
craft: From the battlefield, pay the craft cost (often exiling other cards) to exile this and return it transformed.
bargain: You may sacrifice an artifact, enchantment, or token as you cast it for a bonus.
scavenge: From your graveyard, pay the cost and exile it to put that many +1/+1 counters on a target creature.
unleash: May enter with a +1/+1 counter; if it has one, it can't block.
outlast: {cost}, {T}: Put a +1/+1 counter on this (sorcery speed).
renown: When it deals combat damage to a player, if it isn't renowned put N +1/+1 counters on it and it becomes renowned.
ferocious: Gains a bonus if you control a creature with power 4 or greater.
delirium: Gains a bonus if four or more card types are among cards in your graveyard.
revolt: Gains a bonus if a permanent you controlled left the battlefield this turn.
addendum: Gains a bonus if you cast it during your main phase.
coven: Gains a bonus if you control three or more creatures with different powers.
landwalk: Can't be blocked if the defending player controls a land of the named type.
split second: While this spell is on the stack, players can't cast spells or activate non-mana abilities.
embalm: Exile this from your graveyard and pay the cost to create a token copy (a white Zombie).
eternalize: Exile this from your graveyard and pay the cost to create a 4/4 black Zombie token copy.
partner: You may have two commanders if both have partner.
companion: If your starting deck meets its condition, you may bring it in from outside the game once.
prototype: You may cast it for an alternative cost as a smaller artifact creature with set P/T.
